risk_description: Blinded study personnel (site, Pfizer, other) are exposed to unblinded information
critical_factors: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results|Unblinding
additional_context: The study is double blind, double dummy with capsules. No complications or challenges expected with this preparation/dosing. Additional areas of consideration are the possible unblinding nature of biomarkers; including individual Ige E/G, autoantibodies, BHRA/BAT, hsCRP D- dimer and PK data. Biomarkers will not be reported to investigators or PFE until after the study has ended. There is also a slight risk that the transfer of data to/from the adjudication vendor results in accidental unblinding of data.

risk_description: Private data accidentally or purposefully released beyond the limits described in ICDs and/or privacy exhibits. Individual data privacy/rights requests unfulfilled, not fulfilled as expected or take too long to fulfill. Additional [AP 28Jan26] The risk score and controls for this risk are updated in view of updated information & actions related to the vendor. On 17 Dec 25 in Context a QE investigation meeting where Greenphire/Suvoda was present, the vendor stated they will not commit to automating the delivery of the reports for all types of reports as this would require significant programming effort, additionally they stated they are physically unable to notify Security Level 1 occurrences within 1 hour as required in the MSA, therefore, the initial control for this risk is made Inactive and 2 new controls are created to manage the risk (Agreed in 22Jan26 meeting with Investigator & Patient Financial Operations Lead and Service Lead). NOTE TO STUDY TEAMS: the controls for this risk are being handled at enterprise level (above study) but study teams are asked to keep awareness and escalate to Service Lead in the event of noticing any related Data Privacy issue. [AP 3 Dec 25]: This risk is created based on the 2 recent incidents of the vendor sharing invoicing data containing sites and PI names with external parties and sharing site user information with external parties (QE-202067 & QE-207673). Associated Privacy and data protection breaches that compromise Pfizer Policy and global/ local regulatory requirements; Lack of awareness of Causes requirements by vendors, sites, Pfizer colleagues
additional_context: [AP 27Jan26] Following CAPA implementation, until end of 2026, the Service Lead will confirm with Greenphire/Suvoda during the monthly Quality call that no reported data breaches have occurred.

risk_description: Enrolled participant(s) do not meet protocol eligibility criteria Additional QE 197634 - In August 2025, after Last Subject Last Visit (30-Jun-2025), Database Release (25-Jul-2025), and site closure (29-Jul-2025), one Context site network (one main site, three satellite sites) informed their assigned CRA that an internal quality review conducted on 27-Nov-2024 had identified four participants who did not meet protocol inclusion/exclusion criteria. Three participants did not have active disease at the time of consent/enrollment, and one participant had an active infection within 28 days of consent/enrollment.These participants were enrolled in Oct–Nov 2024, but the site did not communicate their internal findings to the sponsor until Aug 2025. Protocol deviations were also not entered until that time, despite the issues being known since Nov 2024. Incomplete medical history entries in EDC further limited the ability to detect these issues during clinical and data reviews.Because this was an LIS1 study with no SDV requirement and only 10% SDR, the affected participants were not included in the SDR pool and ineligibility was not identified during monitoring.As a result, the final database and analyses included data from ineligible participants. This requires unlocking the database, correcting participant data (including protocol deviations and medical history), and re-running analyses to ensure accuracy of the CSR and any subsequent publications. Associated Inclusion / exclusion criteria not met Causes
additional_context: Due date to be set to end of recruitment period.

Study team is implementing the QTL for Nasal swab obtained at the time of a potential COVID-19 illness (Substudy A Phase 2,3). More specifically cumulative percentage calculated as the cumulative number of nasal swabs obtained from confirmed symptoms triggering illness/telehealth illness consultations divided by the cumulative number of all illness visits with symptoms (which should therefore have a corresponding swab). Until Aug-2025 we are implemented this QTL for SSA for all groups(1-6) but from Sep-2025 we have this only for group 6 (captured in cluepoints).

risk_description: Enrolled participant(s) do not meet protocol eligibility criteria
additional_context: Participants have risk conditions (they could have multiple risk conditions). Importance of the adequate review by the site the participant medical history (including vaccination history). Importance of availability of medical history. Potential for difficulties in enrollment of this population (but we are mitigating this by having many sites activated).

risk_description: Samples incorrectly obtained, analyzed, stored or handled (inclusive of transportation and import / export)
critical_factors: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results|Endpoints

risk_description: Submission / Approval to Health Authorities (HA) is delayed or rejected (includes Health Authorities outside of the US and EU)
critical_factors: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results
additional_context: Challenging Regulatory landscape. On 26 August 2025, the FDA issued revised timelines proposing an accelerated schedule for study activities, despite the original dates already representing the earliest feasible plan. While Pfizer has acknowledged and agree to the updated timelines, the study team has discussed and agreed to maintain the original timelines, with all reasonable efforts directed toward aligning activities as closely as possible with the FDA-agreed milestones. Current internal feasibility does not align with timelines in FDA approval meaning there may be need for partial submissions or request for extensions.

risk_description: Data collected from the trial or reliability of trial results are not as expected
critical_factors: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results|Endpoints
additional_context: Originally 3 QTLs, now only 2 QTLs are still applicable. Applicable QTLs are % of subjects with premature drug discontinuation and % of subjects that have errors in efficacy assessments. The 3rd, % of enrolled patients who do not meet entry criteria is no longer being utilized as all patients have already enrolled.

risk_description: Data collected from the trial or reliability of trial results are not as expected
critical_factors: Endpoints|Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results
control_types: Quality Tolerance Limit (QTL) and Clinical Review
additional_context: In monthly basis, Clinical and Stats will review the number of primary events in blinded fashion. In case the number of events is low, study team will evaluate other mitigations (e.g. revision of protocol design, populations, and/or endpoints). QTL: Percentage of study participants lost to follow-up by Day 28.

risk_description: Private data accidentally or purposefully released beyond the limits described in ICDs and/or privacy exhibits. Individual data privacy/rights requests unfulfilled, not fulfilled as expected or take too long to fulfill. Additional [AP 28Jan26] The risk score and controls for this risk are updated in view of updated information & actions related to the vendor. On 17 Dec 25 in Context a QE investigation meeting where Greenphire/Suvoda was present, the vendor stated they will not commit to automating the delivery of the reports for all types of reports as this would require significant programming effort, additionally they stated they are physically unable to notify Security Level 1 occurrences within 1 hour as required in the MSA, therefore, the initial control for this risk is made Inactive and 2 new controls are created to manage the risk (Agreed in 22Jan26 meeting with Investigator & Patient Financial Operations Lead and Service Lead). NOTE TO STUDY TEAMS: the controls for this risk are being handled at enterprise level (above study) but study teams are asked to keep awareness and escalate to Service Lead in the event of noticing any related Data Privacy issue. [AP 3 Dec 25]: This risk is created based on the 2 recent incidents of the vendor sharing invoicing data containing sites and PI names with external parties and sharing site user information with external parties (QE-202067 & QE-207673). Associated Privacy and data protection breaches that compromise Pfizer Policy and global/ local regulatory requirements; Lack of awareness of Causes requirements by vendors, sites, Pfizer colleagues
control_types: Training on requirements and Third Party management
additional_context: [AP 27Jan26] Following CAPA implementation, until end of 2026, the Service Lead will confirm with Greenphire/Suvoda during the monthly Quality call that no reported data breaches have occurred.

risk_description: Improper or delayed payments / invoices / incentives / benefits
additional_context: From Investigator & Patient Financial Operations activities have started to engage a new vendor and transition out of Greenphire Inc within 2026.

risk_description: Disclosure of company confidential information / intellectual property
additional_context: ADDITIONAL CONTROL: Security Enhancement Initiative - 4 key deliverables: (Six digit PIN requirement on software, Multi-factor authentication, Biometric capability for provisioned and BYOD and Password reset requirement)
